May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts **Source** [Pathfinder #199: To Bloom Below the Web](/Sources.aspx?ID=225) pg. 77 **Price** 1,820 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Base Weapon** [Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=12) --- This ash wood staff is topped by a long tassel of bleached-white horsehair. Historically carried by the most important of court officials throughout various regions and periods of Tian Xia's history, purgatory emissary's staves are also strongly associated with psychopomps serving punitive sentences. It's thought this connection is a comical nod to the bureaucratic nature of the afterlife. Psychopomps and spirits tend to regard you with a level of respect while you carry a _purgatory emissary's staff_. While you wield this staff, you receive a +2 item bonus to all skill checks made to adjust a psychopomp's or spirit's attitude. When used as a weapon, a purgatory emissary's staff is a _+2 striking [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=367)_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list.
  • **Cantrip** [_disrupt undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=79)
  • **1st** [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [_sanctuary_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1660)
  • **2nd** [_remove fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=252), [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271)
  • **3rd** [_searing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=269), [_zone of truth_](/Spells.aspx?ID=379)
  • **4th** [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [_talking corpse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712)
  • **5th** [_banishment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448), [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340)
name: Purgatory Emissary's Staff

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs=""
>
[Skyfang Crystal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3175)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #199: To Bloom Below the Web](/Sources.aspx?ID=225) pg. 77

**Price** 2,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _skyfang crystal_ appears as a softly glowing length of blue crystal shaped like a gently curved fang; it's typically affixed to a short length of chain, allowing it to be worn as a necklace or wrapped around a forearm. The light it sheds is equal in strength to that of a candle; it can't be extinguished but can be blocked simply by wrapping the crystal in cloth, wearing it under clothing, or keeping it in a container. The first of these magical crystals was believed to have been created by a dying sky dragon who'd spent her life fighting against evil spirits. She plucked a dozen of her smallest teeth from her jaw and offered them to her 12 most trusted allies so that they could continue to track down evil spirits and remain protected as they did so, even after she was gone. A _skyfang crystal_ provides illumination as a candle that can't be extinguished, and as long as you have it invested, you gain mental resistance 10. If you're evil, you're enfeebled 2 while you have a _skyfang crystal_ invested. 

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You touch the _skyfang crystal_ to your brow and utter the name of a specific fiend or spirit you seek. If the target is on the same plane as you, the _skyfang crystal_ pulses stronger with light when you face in the approximate direction of the named creature, allowing you to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) that creature. The DC for this check is equal to the creature's Will DC, and you can use Occultism, Religion, or Survival to attempt the check. As long as you're tracking this fiend or spirit, you gain a +2 item bonus to saving throws against effects that creature generates. This effect persists until you activate the _skyfang crystal_ again to track a different target

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Skyfang Crystal

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 25"
    pfs=""
>
[Blackaxe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3176)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=723" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=537" />
<trait label="Cursed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=567" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=676" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=221) pg. 329

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Greataxe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=376)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This potent weapon used by [Treerazer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3218) is a _+4 [greater corrosive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2834) [major striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) obsidian [greataxe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=376)_ that grants a +4 item bonus to attack rolls, deals an extra 2d6 damage to [plants](/Traits.aspx?ID=668), and has the properties of [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2855). It deals an additional die of damage when wielded by Treerazer.

**Activate—Owner’s Authority** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=689))   **Requirements** You aren’t wielding _Blackaxe_ and you are its true owner; **Effect** You sense the world around _Blackaxe_ as though you were in its location and can use any of your innate spells through the link as if it were the source of the spell. If another creature is wielding _Blackaxe_, it must succeed at a DC 50 Will save or be [slowed 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) until it relinquishes the weapon.

**Activate—Owner’s Reclamation** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710))   **Requirements** You aren’t wielding _Blackaxe_ and you are its true owner; **Effect** _Blackaxe_ appears in your hands, teleporting instantly from its prior location.

**Activate—Rejuvenating Deforestation** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [positive](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** Make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against a living tree with _Blackaxe_. If it hits, the tree withers to ash and you heal 250 Hit Points and gain the benefit of a 6th-rank [_sound body_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1679) spell.

**Destruction** Chop a powerful magical tree with _Blackaxe_, and while the sap is still fresh, sever one of [Cyth-V’sug’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=74) limbs. This makes _Blackaxe_ explode violently.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Blackaxe

[Alluring Lantern](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3177)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Graft" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=790" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 97

**Price** 450 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** implanted

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An antenna with a bioluminescent lure protrudes from your head, drawing enemies' attention. When not activated, the antenna lays flat and blends in with your hair or skin. 

**Activate—Raise Lure** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Until the beginning of your next turn, the lure raises above your head and lights up with multicolored flashes that draws creatures closer. Any creature that begins its turn within 20 feet of you must succeed at a DC 23 Will save or become [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) by the lure and must spend at least one of its actions to move toward you. The fascination ends at the end of the creature’s turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Alluring Lantern

    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bioluminescent Stripes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3178)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Graft" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=790" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=640" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 97

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** implanted

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A line of glands embedded in your skin secretes a chemical that glows when it interacts with the air. As a single action, you can activate your bioluminescent stripes to glow with a [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) in a 20-foot radius and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) for the next 20 feet. While you are glowing, you can’t be [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) and you take a –4 item penalty to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) and [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405). You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the glow as a free action.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bioluminescent Stripes

<trait label="Graft" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=790" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 97

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** implanted

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Special cells in your skin can change color to help you blend in with your environment. You gain the listed item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks to [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) and [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404).

**Activate—Background Adaptation** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For 1 minute, you can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) without needing [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) or [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) to do so. This doesn’t allow you to [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) without ending your movement in cover or concealment, however, as your skin’s attempts to match the background as you move produce noticeable rippling waves of color.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</row>

The bonus is +2, and you can use Background Adaptation once per hour.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Distracting Carapace](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3182)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Graft" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=790" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 98

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** implanted

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your skin is studded with pieces of iridescent chitin that ripple like oil on water. When you move your body in a distracting way, your allies can take advantage to move stealthily. When you [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2292) an ally who is trying to [Create a Diversion](/Actions.aspx?ID=2387), instead of the usual effects of Aid, you can roll an [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) or [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check and use that result to determine the outcome of the diversion, instead of attempting a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) check.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Distracting Carapace

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sharpened Canines](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3187)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Graft" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=790" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 98

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** implanted

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Some of your teeth have been replaced with those of a larger meat-eating predator. You gain a jaws [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) attack that deals 1d6 piercing damage. These jaws are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sharpened Canines
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 98

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Bleeding Canines
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 98

**Price** 520 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The damage die for your jaws unarmed attack increases to 1d8, and whenever you score a critical hit with your jaws, your target takes 1 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) per weapon damage die.
name: Sharpened Canines

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Slashing Claws](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3188)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Graft" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=790" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 98

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** implanted

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Sharp claws have been grafted to your hands or feet, perhaps extending from your knuckles or the tips of your toes. You gain a claw [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) attack that deals 1d4 slashing damage. These claws are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group and have the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526) and [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602) traits.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Slashing Claws
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 98

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Deadly Slashing Claws
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 98

**Price** 320 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The damage die for your claw unarmed attack increases to 1d6 and it gains the [deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570) trait.
name: Slashing Claws

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spry Sinews](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3189)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Graft" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=790" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 98

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** implanted

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The tendons in your legs are uncommonly stretchy. When you [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298), increase the distance traveled by the listed amount.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Spry Sinews (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 98

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You increase the horizontal distance of your [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) by 5 feet and the vertical distance by 3 feet.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Spry Sinews (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 99

**Price** 1,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You increase the horizontal distance of your [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) by 10 feet and the vertical distance by 6 feet.
name: Spry Sinews

markdown:
**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 99 **Price** 160 gp **Usage** implanted --- Sacs full of dark ink have been implanted inside your mouth, allowing you to spit it forth. **Activate—Spray Ink** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You splatter ink in a 10-foot cone, covering creatures and revealing [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) ones. Each creature in the area must succeed at a DC 20 Reflex save or become covered in ink. If a creature has its invisibility negated by this ink, it is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) instead of invisible. A creature can negate the effects of the ink by spending two Interact actions to wipe off the ink. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target’s invisibility is negated for 2 rounds.
**Failure** The target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round. Its invisibility is negated for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The target is blinded for 10 minutes. Its invisibility is negated for 10 minutes. If this graft is used underwater, you can instead choose to release the ink in a 10-foot emanation. The resulting ink cloud lasts for 10 minutes. All creatures in the ink cloud become concealed, and all creatures outside the ink cloud become concealed to creatures within it.
name: Squid Ink Sac

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hodag Leather](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3196)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 100

**Price** 500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Studded Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=42)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Dyed green and white and studded with the natural spines of a hodag, this _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2785) [deathless](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1238) [studded leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=42)_ is an imposing sight, especially when its wearer is barreling towards you.

**Activate—Hodag Toss** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to your Speed and make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) at the end of your movement. If you succeed at your Strike, attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check against the creature’s Fortitude DC. On a success, the creature is thrown 10 feet in a straight line in the direction of your choice and lands [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). If the creature is knocked into a solid object, it takes 1d6 bludgeoning damage before landing prone. You can instead toss the creature straight up into the air. The creature lands in the same square it occupied, and takes 1d6 bludgeoning damage as it lands prone. You can’t use this ability again for 1d4 rounds.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the hide of a [hodag](/Monsters.aspx?ID=701).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hodag Leather

markdown:
**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 100 **Price** 990 gp **Usage** worn armor **Bulk** 2 **Base Armor** [Scale Mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=45) --- The faint glow beneath the thick, algae-green scales of this suit of _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2785) [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) [scale mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=45)_ hints at the armor’s source. **Activate—Luminous Lure** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The _mamlambo scale_ sheds [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). The light has a duration of 1 minute, but you can extinguish the light with an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action. A creature that begins its turn in the light must attempt a DC 26 Will save. Regardless of the save’s result, the creature is temporarily immune for 1 hour. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72), and for as long as it remains in the light, it must spend at least 1 of its actions on each of its turns to move closer to the source of the light. If the creature ends its movement adjacent to you, it is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 until it is out of the light or until you make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against it.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is also [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against the first Strike you make against it while it is slowed. **Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the scales and hide of a [mamlambo](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1439).

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 101

**Price** 340 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</row>

</column>

---

This _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [scythe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=394)_ is made from the cruel, blackened branches of the carnivorous scythe tree, which hungrily drink up spilled blood.

**Activate—Root in Blood** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** Your last action was a successful Strike with this weapon and your target isn’t currently taking any persistent bleed damage; **Effect** You break off a piece of the _bloodgorger scythe_ inside your enemy to feed on their blood and sprout into a new tree. The target takes 1 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) at the end of their turn. Each failure to stop the bleeding increases this bleed damage by 1 as a small scythe tree grows from the wound, to a maximum of 3 persistent bleed damage.

**Activate—Sapling Slash** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))   **Requirements** A target has a piece of the _bloodgorger scythe_ broken off inside them and has failed to recover from the persistent bleed damage for 3 consecutive turns; **Effect** The sprouted scythe tree grows large enough to be commanded. It makes a melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against the target or a creature adjacent to the target, with a +18 to hit and dealing 1d10 slashing damage.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include branches and roots from a [scythe tree](/Monsters.aspx?ID=797).

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Chimera Flail](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3205)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 102

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [War Flail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=402)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The three spiked heads of this _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [war flail](/Weapons.aspx?ID=402)_ are fashioned to resemble the heads of a chimera. Preserved fragments of bone from the creature are fused with the metal of each head, replacing the typical weight at the end of the chain. Each head has been shrunk and preserved for use. You can have only one head slotted at a time and can use only the ability of the currently slotted head, while the other two hang off the pommel. Switching between heads requires an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action. 

 

<title level="2" right="">Dragon</title> This head does piercing damage instead of the normal bludgeoning damage, plus 1 damage type based on the magical tradition of the chimera’s dragon head. (**Arcane** force; **Divine** spirit; **Occult** mental; **Primal** acid, cold, electricity, fire, sonic, or poison, chosen at item creation)

**Activate—Bursting Breath** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes;  **Requirements** your last action was a successful [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306); **Effect** You tap into the residual energy of the dragon head and release it as a small breath explosion. This does 2d6 damage of the dragon’s additional damage type in a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) centered around one corner of the space the target occupies. After using this, the dragon head attachment loses its additional damage type. You can spend 10 minutes to rest and recharge the head.

 

<title level="2" right="">Lion</title> This head does piercing damage instead of the normal bludgeoning damage, plus 1 additional precision damage if the target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58).

**Activate—Pouncing Whirl** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes;  **Requirements** you have not made an attack this turn; **Effect** You tap into the predator speed of a lion. You [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to half your Speed and make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against a target. If that creature is within reach of an ally, regardless if they would normally provide [flanking](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375) or not, that creature is considered [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to your Strike. Your additional precision damage against an off-guard creature increases to 1d4. After using this, the lion head loses its additional precision damage. You can spend 10 minutes to rest and recharge the head.

<title level="2" right="">Goat</title> If your last action was to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) at least half your Speed, this does an additional 1d4 force damage.

**Activate—Charging Horns** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes;   **Effect** You swing the hammer around with the full force of a charging ram. Make a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against up to three enemies within your reach. The first two Strikes are at your current attack bonus, and the third is with the normal attack penalty. On a successful hit, the target takes an additional 1d6 force damage and is [pushed back](/Rules.aspx?ID=2364) 5 feet, or 10 feet on a critical hit. After using this, the goat head loses its additional force damage. You can spend 10 minutes to rest and recharge the head.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include all three heads from a [chimera](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2879).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Chimera Flail

The weights of this _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [meteor hammer](/Weapons.aspx?ID=296)_ are shaped like snake heads, and anaconda scales adorn the chain.

**Activate—Coiling Chain** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))   **Requirements** Your last action was a successful melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382) with the _constricting meteor_; **Effect** The chain of the meteor hammer wraps around the target and squeezes. Attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) the creature with a +1 item bonus. In addition to being grabbed on a success, the target takes 1d10+7 bludgeoning damage.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the scales of a [giant anaconda](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3203).

Three dried tentacles from a giant squid studded with pufferfish spines have been twisted together to form this robust _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [wounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2854) [whip](/Weapons.aspx?ID=404)_. With a clever flick of the wrist, they can be unfurled into a deadly arc of poisonous barbs.

**Activate—Poison Barrage** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You lash out with the whip’s three tentacles in a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). Each creature inside the cone must attempt a DC 27 basic Fortitude save or take 4d6 poison damage. On a critical failure, the creature also takes 1d6 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include three tentacles from a [giant squid](/Monsters.aspx?ID=820).

[Splithead Bow](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3209)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 103

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=436)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [hauling](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1372) [longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=436)_ is lined with the flesh of a fallen [hydra](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3064) and ornate carvings on the grip that depict the hydra’s number of heads it had before it was slain. When you wield the bow in combat, you regain 2 Hit Points at the start of each of your turns.

**Activate—Two-Headed Arrow** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You imbue your arrow with the properties of a hydra, splitting it in two and sending each at a different foe. Make two ranged [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306), each against a separate target. Both targets must be in your line of sight, and within 50 feet of you. Both Strikes count toward your multiple attack penalty, but the penalty doesn’t increase until after you’ve made both attacks. Additionally, after using this ability, you do not regain Hit Points from wielding the bow on your next turn.

**Activate—Five-Headed Arrow** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You make a powerful [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) propelled by the ferocity of a hydra at a target within your first range increment. If your Strike is successful, the arrow deals damage as normal and then passes through the target, splitting into four pieces. Choose four other targets within 30 feet of the original target and make a single ranged Strike with your multiple attack penalty, comparing the result to each secondary target’s AC. After using this ability, you do not regain Hit Points from wielding the bow until after your next daily preparations.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the flesh from a severed head of a [hydra](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3064).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Splithead Bow

<column gap="tiny">

The beak and talons of a tidehawk form the triggering mechanism of this [+2](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [crossbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=425). Its grip is constantly damp, though this doesn’t affect your ability to keep ahold of the weapon. When used in aquatic combat, a _tidal crossbow_ doesn’t have its range increments halved. A _tidal crossbow_ deals 1d4 additional bludgeoning damage on a successful Strike. On a critical hit, the bow’s additional damage is instead 1d4 bludgeoning splash damage, and the target must succeed at a DC 31 Reflex save or be pushed back 5 feet. The bow critical specialization effect applies to creatures with the water trait even if it wouldn’t normally.

**Activate—Typhoon Swell** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The tidal crossbow produces a torrent of water in a 30-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388), dealing 8d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 30 basic Fortitude save). Creatures that fail the save are also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the beak, talons, and several watery feathers of a [tidehawk](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1337).

</column>

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 103 **Price** 140 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 2 **Base Weapon** [Greatpick](/Weapons.aspx?ID=378) --- This _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [greatpick](/Weapons.aspx?ID=378)_ bears a head studded with the tusks of a [trollhound](/Monsters.aspx?ID=835). **Activate—Ravenous Strike** ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Effect** You Strike with the _trollhound pick_, transferring a portion of a trollhound’s legendary appetite to the target. A living enemy who takes damage from the attack must attempt a DC 19 Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature suffers hunger pangs, becoming offguard for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature experiences gnawing hunger, becoming [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 round and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 1 minute. If the creature consumes any amount of food or drink (including imbibing a potion), the enfeebled condition ends.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is enfeebled 2 and each instance of consuming food or drink reduces the penalty by 1. **Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the teeth and tongue from a [trollhound](/Monsters.aspx?ID=835)

>
[Whip-Tongue Sling](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3213)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 104

**Price** 1,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Sling](/Weapons.aspx?ID=430)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This is a _[+2](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [sling](/Weapons.aspx?ID=430)_ made from the neck, jaw, and tongue of a [mobogo](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1229) to resemble its mouth. The tongue curls around each bullet, which is thrown with the snap of your wrist. Each throw causes a small croak to echo from the sling.

**Activate—Bolstering Croak** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You raise your sling and work the handle to mimic a mobogo’s croaking song. Allies within 25 feet gain a +1 status bonus to saves against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) effects for 1 round. You can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) this for up to 4 rounds. During this time, you can’t use the sling to [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) or activate its Tongue Twister ability. After using Bolstering Croak, you can recharge it once per day by feeding the sling a number of gallons of water equal to double the number of rounds for which the Bolstering Croak was active. For example, if you activated but didn’t sustain Bolstering Croak, you would require 2 gallons of water to recharge it. If you activated Bolstering Cloak and then Sustained for 1 round, you would require 4 gallons of water.

**Activate—Tongue Twister** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** The sling isn’t loaded; **Effect** You fully unfurl the sling’s tongue to grab hold of a creature within 20 feet, and attempt to move them. Attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check against the creature’s Fortitude DC. On a success, you can move the creature up to 15 feet into any space within 20 feet of you. If you target a willing ally with this ability, you automatically succeed at the Athletics check.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the throat, tongue, and skin from a [mobogo](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1229).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Whip-Tongue Sling

The stock and barrel of this _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [gun sword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=218)_ are lined with scales from a [storm snake](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3314). Their insulating properties grant their wielder some protection against electricity.

**Activate—Lightning Rod** <actions string="Reaction" /> ;   **Trigger** You take electricity damage;  **Effect** You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to the triggering damage, and the _fulmination fang_ crackles with the absorbed energy. You can [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) to change between the gun sword’s current usage (melee to ranged, or ranged to melee). The next melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) you make with the fulmination fang within the next minute deals an additional 1d6 electricity damage. After a minute, the absorbed energy dissipates.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the scales of a [storm snake](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3314).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fulmination Fang

    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Howler Pistol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3216)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 104

**Price** 1,100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Dragon-Mouth Pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=200)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Designed by a gunsmith as a personal challenge, a _howler pistol_ strikes a miniature version of the [sonic horn](/SiegeWeapons.aspx?ID=29) siege weapon’s resonating orb with its hammer to generate beams of sonic energy. A _howler pistol_ is a _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [dragon mouth pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=200)_ that deals sonic damage instead of piercing damage and doesn’t have the [concussive](/Traits.aspx?ID=401) trait.

**Activate—Resonant Shatter** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** The _howler pistol_ is loaded; **Effect** You over-torque the _howler pistol_’s hammer to strike the resonating orb harder than normal. The gun fires a concentrated burst of sonic energy that shatters a nearby stone or rock surface, creating a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) at a point of impact within the gun’s first range increment. Creatures in the area take 5d6 piercing damage (DC 27 basic Reflex save).

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the powdered horn from a [soniphak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3269), processed into a resonating orb.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Howler Pistol

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 104 **Price** 150 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 **Base Weapon** [Hand Cannon](/Weapons.aspx?ID=193) --- This _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [hand cannon](/Weapons.aspx?ID=193)_ is crafted by wrapping the water-producing organ cluster of a [grodair](/Monsters.aspx?ID=694) around its barrel, eliminating the need for ammunition, though the living tissue must be maintained with 1 sp of specialized nutrient feed each day. If not, its misfire check is DC 10. If you fail this misfire check, the organ bursts, dealing an amount of bludgeoning damage equal to double the number of weapon damage dice to you and rendering the _hydrocannon_ broken. **Activate—Tinker Shot** ([manipulation](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Effect** You fiddle with the pressure regulator on the _hydrocannon_ and then make a ranged [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with the gun. A successful attack has an additional effect depending on which modular configuration the _hydrocannon_ is currently in.
  • **Bludgeoning** The target is pushed 5 feet away from you; this [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=2364) is increased to 10 feet on a critical hit.
  • **Piercing** The target takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds for 1 round.
  • **Slashing** The target takes 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).
**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the extradimensional organ cluster extracted from a [grodair](/Monsters.aspx?ID=694). Harvesting the organs of a grodair in this way differs greatly from the normal method; it takes an hour and a successful DC 22 [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) check. On a critical failure, the organ cluster bursts and deals 4d6 bludgeoning damage to the harvester.
name: Hydrocannon

The larynx of a [leydroth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=719), a dangerous creature whose roar can unravel magic, is integrated into the firing mechanism of this _[+3](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [blunderbuss](/Weapons.aspx?ID=196)_.

**Activate—Dispelling Blast** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _leydroth spellbreaker_ emits a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of dispelling energy. Attempt a single [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) check at +25 against each spell or magical effect in the area, plus one item or effect on each creature in the area, with the effects of [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493).

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the larynx of a [leydroth](/Monsters.aspx?ID=719).

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />

</row>

</row>

</column>

---

Ritually constructed from the bones of a [nightmare](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3105), a _nightmare’s lament_ is a _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [rapier pistol](/Weapons.aspx?ID=222)_. Thin wisps of dark smoke continually surround the redflecked blade.

**Activate—Fuming Cloak** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For 1 minute, the _nightmare’s lament_ continually vents thick, black smoke that creates [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) in a 15-foot aura around the gun’s wielder. The wielder of the nightmare’s lament can see through this smoke. A creature that begins its turn in the area becomes [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 2 (DC 23 Fortitude negates) and is then temporarily immune to the sickness from the smoke for 1 minute. The wielder of the _nightmare’s lament_, any creature currently holding its breath (or that does not need to breathe), and any creature immune to poison are immune to the aura’s sickened effect but not the concealment.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the rib bones and inner fire of a [nightmare](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3105).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Nightmare's Lament

**Activate—Web Chord** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** Sticky webbing sprays in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) as you strum the lute’s strings. Each creature in the area of the webbing is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) unless it succeeds at a DC 29 Reflex save.

**Craft Requirements** The initial materials must include the spinnerets of a [goliath spider](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3209), as well as a spool of its webbing.

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 105 **Price** 2,600 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 2 --- The main portion of this set of bagpipes is fashioned from the dried skin of a [cauthooj](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2870), with the feathers still attached. The bird’s vocal cords are crafted into the instrument’s reeds. The pipes grant a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks while playing music with them. **Activate—Disorienting Fugue** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** You play several notes on the pipes, quickly altering their pitch and tone. Each creature within a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 31 Will save to resist the song. Creatures that attempt this save become temporarily immune to Disorienting Fugue for 1 minute. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected and its temporary immunity to Disorienting Fugue increases to 1 hour.
**Success** The target is unaffected.
**Failure** The target is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target is confused for 1 round and immediately attacks itself (in the normal fashion for attacking oneself while confused). This [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) doesn’t give the creature a flat check to recover from the confusion. **Activate—Throw Song** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet attempts a melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against you or an ally; **Effect** You let loose a staccato chirp from the pipes that appears to come from somewhere else. The triggering creature must attempt a DC 31 Will save. On a failure, the creature redirects the Strike to another creature of your choice within reach of the melee Strike. If no other creatures are within range, the affected creature instead takes a –2 penalty to the Strike. **Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the skin and vocal cords of a [cauthooj](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2870).
name: Cauthooj Bagpipes

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 106 **Price** 50 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 2 --- This unassuming hooded lantern contains a mass of flash beetle eggs suspended in a magical solution. It sheds light on a 45-foot radius (and dim light in the next 45 feet). **Activate—Spotlight** ([light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Flipping a concealed lever in the lantern’s handle triggers a small current to pass through the solution. The eggs brighten and emit a series of brilliant flashes in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). Each creature in the area of effect must attempt a DC 18 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is blinded for 1 minute. **Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the eggs of a [flash beetle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2852).

Your hands fit through the venomous mouths of this pair of handwraps made from [amphisbaena](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1060) skin. Your unarmed strikes gain the [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724) trait. _Amphisbaena handwraps_ can have weapon runes etched onto them, similar to [_handwraps of mighty blows_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3086).

**Activate—Twin Venom Strike** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** Make two unarmed [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306). Both Strikes count toward your [multiple attack penalty](/Rules.aspx?ID=2289), but the penalty doesn’t increase until after both attacks. Each Strike deals an additional 1d6 poison damage.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the skin and heads of an [amphisbaena](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1060).

    right="Item 7"

>
[Amulet of the Hellcat](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3224)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 106

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This glass amulet is set on a steel backing shaped like a cage and flickers with an eerie crimson glow. Magically sealed within is a portion of a hellcat's ever-burning heart. While wearing the amulet, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks made while in [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=407), and you can cast the [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585) cantrip emanating from the amulet as an [innate](/Rules.aspx?ID=2232) divine spell.

**Activate—Invisible Pounce** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You are in bright light; **Effect** You become [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83), [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to your speed, and then make a melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) at the end of that movement. After making the Strike, you are no longer invisible. If at any point during your Stride, you pass out of [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402), you are no longer invisible and must stop moving. You may make a melee Strike if you have a target within reach.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the heart of a [hellcat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=695).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Amulet of the Hellcat

<traits>

<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />

<row gap="medium">

</row>

---

This soft covering for the mouth and nose is made from the sleek fur of a [dweomercat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=643) and is fastened with two gnarled teeth of the same beast. Each day during daily preparations, you must brush and oil the veil. At the conclusion of the activity, select one tradition of magic ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), or [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)). You gain a +1 item bonus to all saves against magic from that tradition until your next daily preparations.

**Activate—Dimension Leap** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentration](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Frequency** once per day; Trigger You succeed at a saving throw against a spell from the chosen tradition;   **Effect** Your veil billows out and you disappear behind it. You use the traces of [dweomercat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=643) magic to teleport yourself instantly to any unoccupied square within 30 feet. If you critically succeeded at the triggering save, you can instead teleport within 45 feet.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the hide and teeth of a [dweomercat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=643).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dweomerveil

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mantle of the Tikbalang](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3226)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 106

**Price** 425 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This long hide cloak is stitched with patches of the ebony hair of a [tikbalang](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1338). While wearing this cloak, you take a –2 item penalty to saves against illusions but gain a +2 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks.

**Activate—Illusory Thrash** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You wrap the mantle around your body, causing you to briefly appear much larger than you are. Make a melee Strike. This Strike deals an additional 4d6 mental damage.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the hide of a [tikbalang](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1338).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mantle of the Tikbalang

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Orm Choker](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3227)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 106

**Price** 950 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn collar

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This elaborate choker is fashioned from the treated hide of a [water orm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=856). Its interior is lined with the creature’s silky hair. While wearing an orm choker, your form blurs slightly around the edges, granting you a +1 item bonus to saving throws against [detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=574), [revelation](/Traits.aspx?ID=686), and [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=689) effects.

**Activate—Watery Form** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentration](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You dissolve into liquid, appearing only as a stretch of flowing water. While in this form, you gain a [swim Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2351) of 45 feet, you automatically succeed at [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381), and you gain a +4 circumstance bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks in water. However, you can’t speak, use any of your other items or abilities, or enter a body of salt water while in this form. You can remain in this form for up to 1 hour, though you can return to your normal form using a single action that has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait.

**Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include the hide and hair of a [water orm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=856).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Orm Choker

markdown:
**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 108 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**1 minute ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) --- These chemicals convey myriad signals. Many varieties of these alchemical cocktails exist, each tailored for the unique chemistry of a specific type of animal. You can, for example, make wolf pheromones, but not pheromones that affect all animals. The pheromones don’t work on creatures that are similar to the specific kind of animal, but aren’t actually animals—for example, wolf pheromones don’t work on werewolves. When you learn the formula for animal pheromones, you learn the formulas for all common animals. If no animals of a kind are common, you must learn the formula for it separately, and the formula has the same rarity as the least-rare creature of that kind. You Activate animal pheromones by rubbing them on yourself or a creature within reach. The pheromones last for 24 hours or until scrubbed clean with 1 minute of work (consisting of multiple [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) actions). During this time, the pheromones exude a subtle aroma in a 15-foot radius that calms and influences the designated animals. A designated animal that ends its turn within the affected area must attempt a Will saving throw with the following effects. The animal is then temporarily immune to the pheromones for 24 hours. The effect lasts for the duration of the pheromones, even after the creature leaves the affected area. **Critical Success** The animal is unaffected. It doesn’t necessarily become aware of the pheromones, although intelligent animals might become suspicious if they observe others of their kind being affected.
**Success** The animal’s [attitude](/Rules.aspx?ID=2455) toward the affected creature improves by one step. If this improves its attitude to at least [indifferent](/Conditions.aspx?ID=82), it can’t take [hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251) against the affected creature, though the change in attitude ends as soon as the affected creature takes a hostile action against the animal or its allies.
**Failure** As success, but the attitude increases by two steps.
**Critical Failure** As success, but the attitude increases to [helpful](/Conditions.aspx?ID=78). **Craft Requirements** Provide fur or a similar tissue sample from the designated kind of animal. Animal Pheromones (Lesser) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 108 **Price** 30 gp --- The DC of the save is 19. Animal Pheromones (Moderate) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 108 **Price** 300 gp --- The DC of the save is 27. Animal Pheromones (Greater) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 108 **Price** 3,000 gp --- The DC of the save is 35.
name: Animal Pheromones

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Aroma Concealer](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3229)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Oil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=663" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 109

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><row>1 minute ([Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This oily mix of herbs and natural detergents can be applied to a creature to reduce and cover any ordinary odors they produce. The creature receives a +2 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) or [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) against creatures using primarily smell. This bonus also applies to the DC to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) the creature by scent. The listed amount is enough to cover one Medium or smaller creature and takes 1 minute to apply. The effect lasts for 1 hour after applying.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Aroma Concealer

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 109 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 1 **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) --- This alchemical candle is attached to a tall pole and grounding stake. When the flare is lit, it sprays a concentrated cloud of pheromones to attract the attention of a specific kind of animal (see [alchemical pheromones](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3228)). Similar to animal pheromones, when you learn the formula for a pheromone flare, you learn the formulas for all common animals. A pheromone flare has a built-in delayed activation timer of up to 1 hour. When it's activated, the flare releases a cloud of pheromones in a 60-foot emanation that lasts for 1 hour. A strong wind reduces the emanation to 15 feet for as long as the wind lasts. Moving the flare after it has been activated ends the effect. A designated animal that enters the area must attempt a Will save with the following effects. Animals with an Intelligence modifier of –3 or higher increase the result of their save by one step. **Critical Success** The animal is unaffected and temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Success** The animal is unaffected but must attempt a new save every 10 minutes it remains within the area.
**Failure** The animal is attracted by the pheromones, following the cloud to its source. The creature becomes [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) until it reaches the flare and spends 1 round investigating it. After this, it becomes temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the fascination lasts for up to 1 hour, though the animal can attempt a new save against the effect every 10 minutes. The animal takes a –1 status penalty to this save that increases by 1 with each subsequent failure or critical failure, up to a maximum of a –3 status penalty Pheromone Flare (Lesser) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 109 **Price** 30 gp --- The DC of the save is 19. Pheromone Flare (Moderate) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 109 **Price** 300 gp --- The DC of the save is 27. Pheromone Flare (Greater) **Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 109 **Price** 3,000 gp --- The DC of the save is 35.
name: Pheromone Flare

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hive Mother Bottle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3231)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Expandable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=475" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 109

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Two Actions" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This bottle is packed with dirt, though the shrunken corpse of an [ankhrav hive mother](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2823) is buried within. When you open the bottle, the effigy of a Huge ankhrav hive mother emerges, burrows up to 20 feet, and then erupts from the ground spraying acid, creating a 15-foot-by-15-foot pit that’s 10 feet deep. Creatures standing in this area take 6d6 acid damage and 1d6 [persistent acid damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) (DC 24 basic Reflex save). A success or critical success means the creature also leaps to safety, landing in the nearest space adjacent to the pit. A failure or critical failure means the creature falls into the pit. Climbing out of the pit requires a successful DC 22 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check.

**Craft Requirements** Supply the corpse of an [ankhrav hive mother](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2823).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hive Mother Bottle

markdown:
**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 109 **Price** 50 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) --- A layer of vapor swirls just below the surface of this glass container holding a large, dried heap of fungal-encrusted seaweed. You can throw the phial up to 30 feet when you Activate it. When opened or thrown, a Medium [sargassum heap](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3171) effigy reconstitutes to release a hallucinogenic cloud in a 15-foot emanation, forcing each creature to attempt a DC 20 Fortitude save. On a failure, a creature rolls 1d4 to determine what it is hallucinating for the next 1d4 rounds (unless otherwise noted). At the beginning of their turn, an affected creature attempts a new save.
  1. The target thinks they are sinking through the floor. It falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) and spends 1 action on its next turn flailing its limbs as if attempting to swim.
  2. The target thinks an item they are holding turns into a viper. The target [Releases](/Actions.aspx?ID=2300) the item and spends its next turn [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) from it.
  3. The target thinks they have shrunk to 1/10 their normal size. For 1 round, it is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 2, [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2, and takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speed.
  4. The target believes they are melting. It drops everything it’s holding and becomes slowed 2 and [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 2 for 1 round.
**Craft Requirements** Supply the corpse of a [sargassum heap](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3171).
name: Sargassum Phial

---

The dried, shrunken corpse of a [giant tarantula](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3208) inside this smoked glass bottle is obscured by swirling vapor and cobwebs. You can throw the ampoule up to 30 feet when you Activate it. When you open or throw the ampoule, a Large giant tarantula effigy bursts forth and lunges at one adjacent creature. If the target fails a DC 21 Fortitude save, it takes 1d10+4 piercing damage. If the target critically fails the save, it’s also exposed to [tarantula venom ](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3208).

**Craft Requirements** Supply the corpse of a [giant tarantula](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3208).

</title>

<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />

Once you imbibe this bitter elixir, your skin exudes a toxin for 1 hour, affecting any creature that hits you with a jaws [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) or other bite attack. If you are [Swallowed Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77) by another creature, they are automatically exposed to the poison every round and take a –2 penalty to their saving throw against it.

 **Frogskin Tincture Poison** ([poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 22 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d4 poison damage (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d6 poison damage and [enfeebled 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) (1 round); **Stage 3** 3d6 poison damage, enfeebled 3, and [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) (1 round)

    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hydra Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3235)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=116" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=670" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 110

**Price** 600 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You sprout a second head, increasing your awareness, intuition, and cognitive ability but also causing your physical capabilities to be impaired as both minds struggle to control a single body.

**Benefit** For 10 minutes, you gain [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) and a +3 item bonus to all Intelligence- and Wisdom-based skill checks. Once during the mutagen's effect, if you fail or critically fail a Will saving throw against a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effect, you can treat the result as one degree of success better.

**Drawback** You are [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 for 10 minutes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hydra Mutagen

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />

</row>

---

Your eyes bulge from their sockets and extend upwards from your head on long, prehensile stalks, greatly enhancing your eyesight and field of view but leaving you unable to close your eyes, increasing your vulnerability to harmful visual effects.

**Benefit** You gain [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49), a +2 item bonus to visual [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) checks, and [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2411) if you don’t already have it. These effects last for 1 minute.

**Drawback** For 1 minute, you take a –2 penalty to saving throws against effects that have the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) trait.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ommatophoric Mutagen

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />

You gain unparalleled sensitivity to the tiniest of vibrations through solid surfaces and even the very air around you, but your eyes become useless.

**Benefit** For 1 minute, you gain [precise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2406) echolocation out to 40 feet, [imprecise](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407) [tremorsense](/Rules.aspx?ID=2413) out to 60 feet, and a +2 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) checks using either of these senses.

**Drawback** You are [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Viperous Elixir](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3238)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=589" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=657" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=669" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 111

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your teeth elongate into fangs with grooves that can channel a deadly venom. You gain a fangs unarmed attack that deals 1d6 piercing damage, unless you already have an unarmed bite attack that deals more damage. You gain the listed item bonus to unarmed attack and damage rolls when you [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with your fangs or another bite attack.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Viperous Elixir (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 111

**Price** 25 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +1 item, your fangs inflict [black adder venom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2892), and the duration is 1 minute or until you hit and deal damage with your fangs, whichever comes first.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>
Viperous Elixir (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 111

**Price** 85 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +2, your fangs inflict [giant scorpion venom](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2899), and the duration is 10 minutes or until you hit and deal damage with your fangs twice, whichever comes first.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Viperous Elixir (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 111

**Price** 225 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +3, your fangs inflict [wyvern poison](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2909), and the duration is 10 minutes or until you hit and deal damage with your fangs three times, whichever comes first.
name: Viperous Elixir

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Injury" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=635" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=669" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 111

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This poison is extracted carefully from a mandragora's thorny vines in a process that, due to the mandragora's humanoid form, eerily mirrors drawing blood.

**Saving Throw** DC 21 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 1d6 poison damage and [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) (1 round); **Stage 2** 1d6 poison damage, [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63), and stupefied 1 (1 round); **Stage 3** 2d6 poison damage, confused, and stupefied 1 (1 round)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sportlebore Capsule](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3240)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Ingested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=633" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=669" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 111

**Price** 70 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Onset** 1 minute

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tiny capsule of soluble material is filled with a flavorless clear powder mixed with thousands of microscopic eggs laid by insidious parasites known as sportlebores. If the capsule or its contents are swallowed by an unwitting victim, the eggs rapidly hatch inside their unfortunate host, causing great abdominal pain. The target can't recover from the poison's [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) or [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) condition except by magic. After stage 3, the target returns to stage 1, but if it progresses to stage 3 a second time, the damage is doubled.

**Saving Throw** DC 22 Fortitude; **Onset** 1 minute; **Maximum Duration** 6 minutes; **Stage 1** sickened 1 (1 minute); **Stage 2** enfeebled 1 and sickened 1 (1 minute); **Stage 3** 4d6 bludgeoning damage (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save) and enfeebled 2 as the affected target painfully vomits (1 minute)

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sportlebore Capsule

Brave alchemists take great care to capture a tatzlwyrm's poisonous vapor in small vials, typically through a system of compressors that can concentrate their exhalations.

**Saving Throw** DC 15 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 3 rounds; **Stage 1** [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) (1 round); **Stage 2** 2d6 poison damage and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) (1 round); **Stage 3** 4d6 poison damage and enfeebled 1 (1 round)

name: Tatzlwyrm's Gasp

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Animal Blind](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3242)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 112

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Simple blinds consist of a series of poles with a cloth to cover them. The cloth covering is styled to represent the natural surroundings such as green and brown leaf and wood patterns for forests, or gray rocks for underground environments. This cloth is either sheer enough to be seen through on one side, or sometimes has small slits cut in it, allowing someone behind it to see what is going on outside while remaining hidden. While not the most convincing camouflage, it's good enough to fool many animals and creatures with lower intelligence.

Animal blinds take 1 minute to set up. Up to two Medium creatures or one Large creature may use the blinds at the same time. You can [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=2307) behind the blind to gain [standard cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459). If the environment matches the terrain depicted on the blind, your circumstance bonus on [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) increases to +4.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 0"
pfs="Standard"
>
Animal Blind
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 2

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Standard"
>
Portable Animal Blind
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 112

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 1

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

Portable animal blinds were originally used for hunting fowl, but resourceful adventurers quickly realized the many possible applications for them. Portable blinds are made of a wooden frame with camouflage material stretched over them, resembling a large kite. A hole in the center allows for a one‑handed ranged weapon to be fired without obstruction. A single person can use the [Take Cover](/Actions.aspx?ID=2307) action while carrying a portable blind. When you do so, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) instead of the normal benefits of standard cover.
name: Animal Blind

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Animal Call](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3243)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 112

**Price** 5 sp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Animal calls are often whistles or similar devices that imitate the calls of [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531). Each call is for a specific type of animal, such as a duck or bear. When you use the call, it gives you a +1 item bonus to [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2400), provided the animal is of the type as the call. You also do not take a circumstance penalty when attempting to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) that animal for not sharing a language.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Animal Call

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Atmospheric Breathing Suit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3244)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 112

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A combination of waterproof leather, durable glass, and a system of copper tubes allows aquatic creatures who wear this suit to be able to spend time on land. They’re custom‑tailored to fit a wide variety of body types and needs. Part of the system of tubes is set up in a way to allow the wearer to speak and hear as well as they normally would with creatures outside the suit. Water, either fresh or salt, is kept moving throughout the system to keep the wearer moisturized. The moving water allows for easier flow to the gills or breathing apparatus of the creature. It also means that the water will slowly run out. Each suit must be refilled with the proper type of water for a creature every 24 hours. It takes 2 gallons of water to refill a suit.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Atmospheric Breathing Suit
