<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Catch Pole](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3245)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 112

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This sturdy pole has a rope attached to one end in a loop with the other end extending to the handle. You can pull the handle side of the rope to tighten the loop. Using this loop, you can [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) without having a free hand. A creature grappled this way receives a –2 circumstance penalty to attack rolls when [Striking](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with an unarmed attack. Due to limitations in the size of the loop, a catch pole can only be used on creatures sized Medium or smaller.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 0"
pfs="Standard"
>
Catch Pole
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 112

**Price** 2 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Giant Catch Pole
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 112

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Bulk** 2

</row>

</column>

---

A giant catch pole is made from thicker steel and heavier rope. It functions the same as a catch pole but can be used to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) creatures up to your normal size limit. However, the implement is so ungainly that you are [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) while wielding it.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Superior Catch Pole
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Howl of the Wild](/Sources.aspx?ID=226) pg. 112

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Hands** 2

**Bulk** 1

</row>

</column>

---

A superior catch pole has a pole made of dawnsilver, and the loop at the end is silk rope, making it sturdier and more agile. It functions the same as a catch pole but gives you a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks made to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=2376) with it.
name: Catch Pole

A flower press resembles a book with wooden covers and straps that tighten down to apply pressure. There are individual pages for multiple plants. Pressing flowers and other plants in this way allows them to be preserved for future study or as ornamentation. It can also be used to make impressions of plants for inclusion in journals and guidebooks instead of illustrations. Most flower presses can contain a dozen or so flowers at a time, each one on a different layer.

---

Made with a brightly colored leather strap, these goggles have a series of overlapping lenses in an array of colors spanning the spectrum of visible light. The effect of this is that every color is more vibrant and similar shades have higher contrast. This means that shapes of even similar colors tend to stand out more, allowing the wearer to see even camouflage easier.

As an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action, you can set the lenses to make things stand out at your current location. Until you move, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) checks when you [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301).

<column gap="tiny">

---

Treated with oil and animal fats, the leather of this pouch is more resistant to water but is also stiffer. Many makers and travelers decorate their oilskin pouches with symbols of the ocean and sailing. Often used to store scrolls or other paper documents when a traveler knows they will be in an area of heavy rain or near water. While not entirely waterproof, an oilskin pouch seals with sturdy leather ties, allowing it to resist anything other than total submersion. Even completely underwater, it will protect its contents for up to 1 minute. 

An oilskin pouch can protect up to 1 Bulk of items.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

---

Most [merfolk](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=74) have no problem surfacing for a while, but constant existence in the air is exhausting and demoralizing. A clever artisan off the coast of Arcadia came up with this solution—a sturdy frame that goes on the shoulders and supports a bubble-like helmet filled with water. A mouthpiece lets the wearer speak to the outside world, and a plant-based filtration system keeps the water at the appropriate level of freshness or brackishness for the wearer. Over the years, these contraptions have spread to most of the world's oceans, though many merfolk don't use them—some find the devices just too heavy and would rather deal with the dryness. The helms are especially popular with abyssal merfolk, who tint the glass dark to protect from the too-bright surface.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Pelagic Helmet

A trundle wheel is used to measure distance. It is a wheel attached to a handle so that it rolls along the ground. The wheel is often covered with leather or a rough metal to allow better grip on a surface and ensure it maintains traction. It’s of an exact circumference, usually a yard or five feet. Each time it makes a complete rotation it makes an audible click so the user knows they have gone exactly that distance and can count the clicks to determine a longer distance traveled.

These relics come in many forms, as the only requirement is they were owned by a human who transformed into a [kushtaka](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1216). Used as a catalyst for [_spirit blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1685) against a target possessing another creature, a _kushtaka relic_ attempts to banish such a spirit. A creature who fails the Fortitude save against _spirit blast_ has its grasp on its possessed target weakened. The result of the possessed creature's next Will save against the possession effect is improved by one degree. A creature who is possessing another and critically fails the Fortitude save against _spirit blast_ takes damage and then is banished from the body it was possessing.

</column>

_Skyfisher vapors_ are only visible via their effect on a [_toxic cloud_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722) spell, or apparent lack thereof. These gases cause the conjured cloud to be as transparent as a [sky fisher](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3310). The cloud is invisible, meaning it does not provide [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62), but it requires a successful [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) check against your [spell DC](/Rules.aspx?ID=2293) to notice the cloud by faint distortions in the air. Your magical connection to the cloud means you always know where it is. This has no effect on its damage.

</column>

The pelt of a [witchwarg](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3231) stays cold long after it leaves the creature's body. A tuft of this fur can freeze a [_fire shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1529) spell into solid ice, inverting its usual effects. The spell's [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) trait is replaced with the [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) trait, you gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) to fire damage and [environmental heat effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820) (instead of cold effects), the shield is immune to cold damage instead of fire damage, and its [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) is halved against fire effects. The damage you deal to creatures that touch you or hit you with a melee or unarmed attack has its type changed to cold.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Witchwarg Fur

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 276 **Price** 350 gp **Bulk** L --- The hide and scales of a dragon can be used to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) any item normally made of ordinary leather or hide. Dragonhide varies in color and texture, depending on the dragon it came from. Due to dragonhide's resiliency, it can also be used to Craft armor usually made out of metal plates (such as a [breastplate](/Armor.aspx?ID=47), [half plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=49), and [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50)), allowing such armor to be made without metal. Dragonhide objects are immune to one damage type, depending on the tradition associated with the dragon. ##

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 278 **Price** 10,000 gp **Usage** worn armor **Bulk** 4 **Base Armor** [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50) --- This suit of _+2 [greater resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) [dragonhide](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3269) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50)_ makes you look like a fearsome dragon. The armor comes in many different varieties depending on the type of dragon from which it's made, though they usually conform to the four magical traditions. **Activate—Dragon Breath** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You unleash a 15-foot cone of energy with a damage type and saving throw corresponding to the tradition of the dragon used to make the armor (shown on the table below). The cone deals 14d6 damage; each creature in the area must attempt a DC 36 basic saving throw ##
Dragon Tradition
Damage
ArcaneForce (Reflex)
DivineSpirit (Reflex)
OccultMental (Will)
PrimalPoison (Fortitude)
 **Craft Requirements** The initial raw materials must include 1,250 gp of dragonhide.
name: Dragonplate

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ghoul Hide](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3272)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 278

**Price** 220 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Hide Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=44)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Stitched together from pieces of [ghoul](/Monsters.aspx?ID=218) skin, this suit of _+1 [hide armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=44)_ grants you a +1 item bonus to saving throws against [curses](/Traits.aspx?ID=566) and makes you immune to the stench of ghouls. Ghoul hide with a [resilient rune](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) increases the resilient rune's item bonus to saving throws against curses by 1 (maximum +4).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ghoul Hide

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 278 **Price** 2,500 gp **Usage** worn armor **Bulk** 1 **Base Armor** [Chain Mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=46) --- This suit of _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) [chain mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=46)_ is made of fine white links of a strange and slightly translucent pale metal, and the sleeves and skirt are fashioned into smaller trails that resemble feathers. Unlike normal chain mail, holy chain has no Speed reduction, its armor check penalty is 0, and its Bulk is 1.
You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC and saving throws against fiends. You appear radiant while you wear the armor, giving you a +2 item bonus to Diplomacy checks against all creatures except fiends.
If you're [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), you're [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 2 while wearing holy chain. You can't recover from this condition while wearing the armor. **Activate—Celestial Flight** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The armor sprouts glowing wings that grant you a fly Speed of 30 feet. The wings shed bright light in a 40-foot radius (and dim light to the next 40 feet). The wings fade away after 10 minutes. **Craft Requirements** You're holy.
name: Holy Chain

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mariner's Splint](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3274)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 278

**Price** 180 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 3
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Splint Mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=48)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This suit of _+1 [splint mail](/Armor.aspx?ID=48)_ is worn by veteran sailors aboard warships who want as much protection as possible but still need to remain mobile enough to [climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) up rigging or [swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) should the need arise. The plates are arranged for maximum flexibility and the undercoat of padded armor is often no more than a cotton shrift. The armor grants you a +1 item bonus to Athletics checks to Climb or Swim and increases the distance you move when you successfully [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) by 5 feet.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mariner's Splint

[Unholy Plate](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3276)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Unholy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=521" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 278

**Price** 2,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 4
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Crafted from black iron, this crude suit of _+2 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) [full plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50)_ is designed to make you look like a horned demon with your face peering out of the screaming maw of the beast. 

While wearing the armor, you can attack with the helmet's horns. They're a martial melee weapon with the effects of a _+2 [weapon potency rune](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830)_. They deal 2d8 piercing damage and have the [deadly](/Traits.aspx?ID=570) d12 and [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) traits. On a critical hit with the horns, the target must succeed at a DC 30 Fortitude or become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (or drained 2 on a critical failure). The horns can't be etched with any runes.

 If you're [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), you're drained 2 and can't recover from this condition while wearing unholy plate. 

**Activate—Demonic Slip** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_translocate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724).

**Craft Requirements** You’re unholy; supply one casting of [_translocate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
